pages.full_text:
the way back to Folly Farm, we are now packing up camp in the sunshine.
    We ended up outside Chesham at __Pedmore__ in Pednore Bottom****
a lovely, small, valley campsite, a cow farm. Chemical W.C. The owner was in hospital.
    It was quite cold, so we decided to all sleep in the van. The sleeping plan was, which
[diagram of van sleeping arrangement: S E on one side, T W / J C / P on other side]
worked quite well, except that we could not reach Charles when he cried. Thomas devised the sleeping plan.
    Charles' birthday tomorrow!
pages.notes: Page continues mid-sentence from previous spread. 'Pedmore in Pednore Bottom' — the first name is underlined; the campsite name/location may read 'Pedmore' / 'Pednore Bottom' — spellings as written. The diagram in the centre of the page shows a van sleeping plan with initials S, E, T, W (or N), J, C, P representing family members' positions. Four asterisks follow 'Pednore Bottom'. 'Pedmore' could alternatively be 'Pedmore' (a place near Stourbridge) but given proximity to Chesham, Bucks, the 'Pednore' reading for the valley/bottom seems more likely — both spellings transcribed as written.

pages.full_text:
__Tuesday__. The local riding stables could not take us, but we found a very good stables __Flamstead__ near Berkhamstead, Ashley Hill.
    Very kind people.
    First we went to a wood-turning factory who may do the long pole of the Madison uplighter at Chesham. Moslems were hugging in the streets because it is the end of Ramadan.
    After lunch on a dog-dirty common, we went to the stables.
    They had tiny Shetland ponies — Becky for John & Monty for Charles — Thomas (Tiger) William (Bunny) Pete — (William) went out for an hour's hack ride, while John (Charles?) rode for ½ hour in the indoor
pages.notes: Page ends mid-sentence (text continues on next spread). 'Flamstead' is underlined — likely Flamstead village near Hemel Hempstead/Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire. 'Berkhamstead' is the diary's spelling (standard modern spelling is Berkhamsted). 'Ashley Hill' may be a local area name near the stables. 'Madison uplighter' appears to be a brand or product name. The parenthetical rider names (Tiger, Bunny) appear to be nicknames. No year is determinable from this page alone; the Ramadan reference and context (volume A, page position) suggest sometime in the 1990s but cannot be confirmed from page content alone.
